documents.name: CyclOps Meeting
documents.excerpt: Explanation of the Numbering System of Antibodies The naming format is: MarkerName_FirstNumber_SecondNumber First number → Indicates clone, fluorophore, and lot. If this number is the same, the samples are from the same clone, same fluorophore, and same lot. If this number changes, it means the lot has changed or it has a different fluorophore 2. Second number → Indicates different vials from the same lot. Even if the first number is the same, a different second number means it’s a different vial of that lot. Explanation of the Numbering System of Antibodies (cont..) Vimentin_1_1 → Vimentin AF750, lot abcdef, vial 1 Vimentin_1_2 → Vimentin AF750, lot abcdef, vial 2 Vimentin_1_3 → Vimentin AF750, lot abcdef, vial 3 Vimentin_2_1→Vimentin AF750, lot mnopq, vial 1 Vimentin_3_1 → Vimentin AF647

document_index.excerpt: Explanation of the Numbering System of Antibodies The naming format is: MarkerName_FirstNumber_SecondNumber First number → Indicates clone, fluorophore, and lot. If this number is the same, the samples are from the same clone, same fluorophore, and same lot. If this number changes, it means the lot has changed or it has a different fluorophore 2. Second number → Indicates different vials from the same lot. Even if the first number is the same, a different second number means it’s a different vial of that lot. Explanation of the Numbering System of Antibodies (cont..) Motivation of SPACE project
Motivation
• Prior work identified tumor-intrinsic determinant (MHCII) of prognosis
• Whether broader tumor genomic and transcriptional states shape the
 baseline TME remains unclear
• Addressing this requires quantification of larger-scale spatial immune
 architecture

Hypothesis
• Distinct tumor-intrinsic genomic and transcriptional states generate distinct
 spatial immune architectures in treatment-naïve HGSC
• These architectures are associated with clinical outcome and therapy
 response

Aim
• Quantify spatial immune architecture in PDS HGSC
• Define tumor-intrinsic states associated with these architectures
• Test associations with prognosis and therapy response

 • SPACE cohort includes 118 primary debulking surgery (PDS) patients with HGSC, enrolled in the Oncosys-

 • Data modalities include:
 • Spatial proteomics (tCycIF) – 114 patients
 • Whole exome sequencing (WES) – 78 patients
 • 40 SNParray (ongoing)
 • Bulk RNA-seq – 107 patients
 • Xenium – 113 patients (ongoing)

 and interface-based immune features across clinical

Beyond localization: immune cells form spatial networks

 • Compartment and interface metrics capture where immune cells are positioned relative to tissue structure
 • However, immune architecture also depends on how immune cells are connected to one another
 • We therefore analyze immune networks to quantify collective spatial organization
 Overview of SPACEstat workflow
• A toolkit allows quantitative assessment at single-cell level or per-slide, focusing on spatial metrics

Simulated CSR provides a null model for network parameter tuning
• We use a complete spatial randomness (CSR) simulation that preserves the tissue scaffold
• This provides a reference for distinguishing biologically structured connectivity from random spatial adjacency
Network distance thresholds are selected using CSR-based component growth
• For each cell type, we assess how the largest connected component grows with increasing distance threshold under CSR
• Rapid component growth at larger distances indicates the onset of artificial connectivity
• We select thresholds below this regime for network construction

CyCLOPS Meeting – 13th February 2025
Summary
Issue 1: Non-Unique Identifiers for CyCIF Slides
Saun raised a concern about the lack of unique identifiers for CyCIF slides. The current naming system follows this format:
Project_Block_Section_Experiment
However, when the same block is sectioned at different times, slide names may overlap. For example, if sample 
S120_iOme
 was sectioned in 
January 2023
 and again in 
January 2025
, the slide name would be identical if the same section is used:
9_S120_iOme_3…
 (potentially duplicated)
Proposed Solution: Unique Slide Identifiers
To ensure uniqueness, a new naming format incorporating the 
time of sectioning
 was proposed to be piloted:
ProjectNumber_Block_TimeOfSection(MonthYear)SectionNumber…
📌 
Examples:
9_S120_iOme_01233…
(sectioned in January 2023)
9_S120_iOme_01253…
(sectioned in January 2025)
Issue 2: Non-Unique Identifiers for Images
Another issue was raised regarding the image naming system. Currently, the 
Experiment
 field does not indicate the final antibody panel or the channels in the image.
Example problem:
S120_iOme section 3
 and 
S121_iOme section 2
 were stained in 
Batch 1
.
S120_iOme section 3
 was successful, resulting in the final image:
9_S120_iOme_01253_1
S121_iOme section 2
 was unsuccessful and rerun in 
Batch 2
 (with the same panel), leading to:
9_S121_iOme_01252_2
❗ However, neither image name provides information about the 
antibodies
 used or final 
channels of image
.
Proposed Solution: Unique Image Identifiers
A new naming format was suggested to include both 
Experiment (Batch)
 and 
Panel (staining details):
ProjectNumber_Block_TimeOfSection(MonthYear)SectionNumber_Experiment(Batch)Panel
📌 
Examples:
9_S120_iOme_01253_1A
(Batch 1, Panel A)
9_S121_iOme_01253_2A

aming format was suggested to include both 
Experiment (Batch)
 and 
Panel (staining details):
ProjectNumber_Block_TimeOfSection(MonthYear)SectionNumber_Experiment(Batch)Panel
📌 
Examples:
9_S120_iOme_01253_1A
(Batch 1, Panel A)
9_S121_iOme_01253_2A
(Batch 2, Panel A)
If additional 
staining cycles
 or 
extra panels
 are applied to the same sample, new raw images would be labelled accordingly:
9_S120_iOme_01253_3B
(Batch 3, Panel B)
Final merged images combining multiple panels would be labelled downstream as:
9_S120_iOme_01253_2AB
(Panels A and B combined)

## Slide 2
Explanation of the Numbering System of Antibodies
The naming format is: MarkerName_FirstNumber_SecondNumber
First number → Indicates clone, fluorophore, and lot.
If this number is the same, the samples are from the same clone, same fluorophore, and same lot.
If this number changes, it means the lot has changed or it has a different fluorophore
2. Second number → Indicates different vials from the same lot.
Even if the first number is the same, a different second number means it’s a different vial of that lot.

## Slide 3
Explanation of the Numbering System of Antibodies (cont..)
Examples
Vimentin_1_1 → Vimentin AF750, lot abcdef, vial 1
Vimentin_1_2 → Vimentin AF750, lot abcdef, vial 2
Vimentin_1_3 → Vimentin AF750, lot abcdef, vial 3
Vimentin_2_1→Vimentin AF750, lot mnopq, vial 1
Vimentin_3_1 → Vimentin AF647, lot ghijk, vial 1
Vimentin_3_2 → Vimentin AF647, lot ghijk, vial
2
Conclusion
Same first number = same clone/fluorophore/lot
Different second number = different vial
Change in first number = new lot Or conjugation status
